description: Day-2 operations concerns that idea-stage briefs and build-focused plans systematically omit: support, monitoring, incident response, backup, training, and maintenance. The Hershey SAP/Siebel 1999 cutover (training and testing prerequisites skipped before a hard go-live date) is the canonical mined failure for this family.

concerns.seed_question: Who answers customer questions and complaints, through which channel, and within what response time?
How do you learn the system is down or degraded before customers tell you?
When the first production incident happens, who is paged, what runbook do they follow, and who can roll back?

concerns.prerequisite_note: A verified restore path must exist BEFORE production data exists; an unverified backup is a hope, not a control.
concerns.seed_question: What is backed up, how often, and when was a restore last actually tested?

concerns.concern: Operator and staff training before cutover
concerns.prerequisite_note: Operator training is predecessor work for any cutover (Hershey SAP/Siebel 1999: training and testing were skipped to hold a hard go-live date, and order fulfillment collapsed in peak season).
concerns.seed_question: Who operates this after launch, and when in the plan do they get trained on it?
Who owns patching, upgrades, and dependency lifecycle after the build team moves on?
